title: Not able to create checkpoint for container in macOS (Apple Silicon)
body:
### Issue Description

Create checkpoint for my container failed

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. create container vm by 
```
podman machine init
```
2. Pull image by
```
podman pull XXX
```
3. I run container by command or by UI
```
docker run --name postgres-db \
    -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
    -p 5432:5432 \
    -d postgres:14.9
```
5. sudo podman container checkpoint <id>
6. get error as below
```
Error: `/usr/bin/crun checkpoint --image-path /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a8130216aeaf867a21117b9c7a4ece8ef91312759973c6e68487889f43a944c3/userdata/checkpoint --work-path /var/home/core/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a8130216aeaf867a21117b9c7a4ece8ef91312759973c6e68487889f43a944c3/userdata a8130216aeaf867a21117b9c7a4ece8ef91312759973c6e68487889f43a944c3` failed: exit status 1
```

7. If ssh on coreOS vm by 
```
podman machine ssh
```
then run command, I got
```
podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                        COMMAND               CREATED       STATUS                   PORTS                     NAMES
a8130216aeaf  container-registry.oracle.com/database/enterprise:19.19.0.0  /bin/bash -c exec...  2 days ago    Up 15 hours (healthy)    0.0.0.0:1521->1521/tcp    oracle-db
01285159b0dc  docker.io/dpage/pgadmin4:latest                                                    39 hours ago  Exited (0) 38 hours ago  0.0.0.0:15080->15080/tcp  pgadmin4
f2a1c44069e5  docker.io/library/postgres:14.9                              postgres              38 hours ago  Up 41 seconds            0.0.0.0:5432->5432/tcp    postgres-db
[core@localhost ~]$ sudo podman container checkpoint postgres-db
Error: no container with name or ID "postgres-db" found: no such container
```

### Describe the results you received

Not very sure whether it's related with below fix:
https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1370
Looks like it is fixed in Fedora CoreOS 37
```
cat /etc/redhat-release 
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
```
similar issue:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12053


### Describe the results you expected

Is anyone know this error ?
I see below in my podman qemu
```
criu --version
Version: 3.17.1
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

My environment is macOS ventura 13.5.1 (Apple Silicon)

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Please provide a full reproducer, how was the container created?
If you use `podman  machine ssh` and then run the checkpoint command there I think it should provide you more information why it failed.

> Please provide a full reproducer, how was the container created? If you use `podman machine ssh` and then run the checkpoint command there I think it should provide you more information why it failed.

I updated reproducer.

If your vm runs rootless mode then checkpoint restore won't work.

You need to run `podman machine init --rootful` to run the podman commands as root user.
Should we look into improving the error message for running checkpoint/restore as non-root Podman? That error is very non-obvious.

> If your vm runs rootless mode then checkpoint restore won't work.
> 
> You need to run `podman machine init --rootful` to run the podman commands as root user.

Thanks all, this resolved the issue.

> Should we look into improving the error message for running checkpoint/restore as non-root Podman? That error is very non-obvious.

There is a check on the client side which is obviously broken. Basically all rootless.IsRootless() calls on the client side are incorrect. I will file a card in backlog as there are much more places were this is a problem.
